description: Combined Ray cluster and training metrics for SageMaker training jobs. Select a SessionName to filter by training job.

panels.description:
Note: not impacted by "Instance" variable.

A total number of active failed, and pending nodes from the cluster. 

ACTIVE: A node is alive and available.

FAILED: A node is dead and not available. The node is considered dead when the raylet process on the node is terminated. The node will get into the failed state if it cannot be provided (e.g., there's no available node from the cloud provider) or failed to setup (e.g., setup_commands have errors). 

Pending: A node is being started by the Ray cluster launcher. The node is unavailable now because it is being provisioned and initialized.
panels.title: Node Count

panels.description: Aggregated utilization of all physical resources (CPU, GPU, memory, disk, or etc.) across the cluster.
panels.title: Cluster Utilization

panels.description: Current state of the train controller.
panels.title: Controller State

panels.description: Time taken by the controller for worker group operations.

targets.legendFormat: Run Name: {{ray_train_run_name}}, Worker Group Start Time
Run Name: {{ray_train_run_name}}, Worker Group Shutdown Time

panels.title: Controller Operation Time

panels.description: Time taken to report a checkpoint to storage.
panels.title: Checkpoint Report Time

panels.description: Node's physical (hardware) GPU usage. The dotted line means the total number of hardware GPUs from the cluster.
panels.title: Node GPU Usage

panels.description: Node's physical (hardware) GPU usage.
panels.title: Node GPU Usage %

panels.description: The physical (hardware) GPU memory usage for each node. The dotted line means the total amount of GPU memory from the cluster.
panels.title: Node GPU Memory Usage (GRAM)

panels.description: The percentage of physical (hardware) GPU memory usage for each node.

targets.expr: sum(ray_node_gram_used{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType, GpuIndex, GpuDeviceName) * 100 / (sum(ray_node_gram_available{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType, GpuIndex, GpuDeviceName) + sum(ray_node_gram_used{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType, GpuIndex, GpuDeviceName))

panels.title: Node GPU Memory Usage (GRAM) %

panels.description: The physical (hardware) CPU usage for each node.
panels.title: Node CPU Usage

panels.description: The percentage of physical (hardware) CPU usage for each node.
panels.title: Node CPU Usage %

panels.description:
The physical (hardware) memory usage for each node. The dotted line means the total amount of memory from the cluster. Node memory is a sum of object store memory (shared memory) and heap memory.

Note: If Ray is deployed within a container, the total memory could be lower than the host machine because Ray may reserve some additional memory space outside the container.
panels.title: Node Memory Usage (heap + object store)

panels.description: The percentage of physical (hardware) memory usage for each node.

targets.expr: sum(ray_node_mem_used{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType) * 100 / sum(ray_node_mem_total{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType)

panels.title: Node Memory Usage % (heap + object store)

panels.description: Network speed per node
panels.title: Node Network

panels.description: Disk IO per node.
panels.title: Node Disk IO Speed

panels.description:
Node's physical (hardware) disk usage. The dotted line means the total amount of disk space from the cluster.

NOTE: When Ray is deployed within a container, this shows the disk usage from the host machine.
panels.title: Node Disk Usage

panels.description:
Node's physical (hardware) disk usage. 

NOTE: When Ray is deployed within a container, this shows the disk usage from the host machine.

targets.expr: sum(ray_node_disk_usage{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType) * 100 / (sum(ray_node_disk_free{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType) + sum(ray_node_disk_usage{instance=~"$Instance", RayNodeType=~"$RayNodeType", SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) by (instance, RayNodeType))

panels.title: Node Disk Usage %

panels.description:
Current count of tasks, grouped by scheduler state (e.g., pending, running, finished).

State: the task state, as described by rpc::TaskStatus proto in common.proto. Task resubmissions due to failures or object reconstruction are shown with (retry) in the label.
panels.title: All Tasks by State

panels.description: Current count of active tasks (i.e. pending or running; not finished), grouped by task name. Task resubmissions due to failures or object reconstruction are shown with (retry) in the label.
panels.title: Active Tasks by Name

panels.description:
Note: not impacted by "Instance" variable.

Current count of actors, grouped by lifecycle state (e.g., alive, restarting, dead/terminated).

State: the actor state, as described by rpc::ActorTableData proto in gcs.proto.
panels.title: All Actors by State

panels.description: Current count of alive actors, grouped by actor name.
panels.title: Alive Actors by Name

panels.description:
Logical CPU usage of Ray. The dotted line indicates the total number of CPUs. The logical CPU is allocated by `num_cpus` arguments from tasks and actors. PENDING means the number of CPUs that will be available when new nodes are up after the autoscaler scales up.

NOTE: Ray's logical CPU is different from physical CPU usage. Ray's logical CPU is allocated by `num_cpus` arguments.

targets.expr: ((sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="CPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) + (sum(autoscaler_pending_resources{resource="CPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) and (sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="CPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) + (sum(autoscaler_pending_resources{resource="CPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) > (sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="CPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)))

panels.title: Logical CPUs Usage

panels.description: Logical GPU usage of Ray. The dotted line indicates the total number of GPUs. The logical GPU is allocated by `num_gpus` arguments from tasks and actors. PENDING means the number of GPUs that will be available when new nodes are up after the autoscaler scales up.

targets.expr: ((sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="GPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) + (sum(autoscaler_pending_resources{resource="GPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) and (sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="GPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) + (sum(autoscaler_pending_resources{resource="GPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)) > (sum(autoscaler_cluster_resources{resource="GPU",SessionName=~"$SessionName",sagemaker_training_job_name=~"$TrainingJobName",instance_type=~"$InstanceType"}) or vector(0)))

panels.title: Logical GPUs Usage

panels.description: Object store memory usage by instance, including memory that has been spilled to disk. The dotted line indicates the object store memory capacity. This metric can go over the max capacity in case of spillage to disk.
panels.title: Object Store Memory Usage

panels.description: Object store memory usage % by instance, including memory that has been spilled to disk. This metric can go over 100% in case of spillage to disk.
panels.title: Object Store Memory Usage %

title: Ray SageMaker Training Job
